editorial: With only 9% cloud cover and a last quarter moon at 34% illumination, tonight offers exceptional astrophotography conditions with minimal light pollution interference. The recent M1.5 solar flare won't produce aurora where you are, but it underscores an active sun worth monitoring as geomagnetic activity remains unsettled. Your real opportunity is wide-open skies and a moon dark enough to let deep-sky targets shine, making this an ideal night to image nebulae, clusters, or galaxies if you have equipment ready. Check your Shoot Score and Your Sky Tonight for how these aggregate conditions translate to your exact location.
